name: Developments in Literacy
mission: Developments in Literacy (DIL) delivers transformative education to underserved children in Pakistan—especially girls—by operating high-quality schools, empowering local educators, and innovating technology solutions to ensure that every child succeeds.
programs: E-Libraries (Read to Grow, Read to Know)
theoryOfChange: Developments in Literacy (DIL) has a published 'Our Approach' document on their website that details their strategic framework. This document explains how their synthesis of technologically driven learning, rigorous teacher training, tailored curricula, and project-based activities enables students to apply concepts and reach their potential. It outlines their two complementary school models (Community Schools and Government Schools) and specific initiatives such as Ed-Tech, Teaching to Teach, Localized Curricula, E-Libraries, and Success Beyond School, all aimed at delivering high-quality education and empowering students, especially girls. Additionally, DIL states that 'The key to the success of DIL's projects is a robust monitoring framework and complete financial transparency,' indicating a structured approach to tracking results. The 'TEAL' program page further illustrates how this specific program aligns quality content, teacher development, and technology 'From strategy to impact' to create measurable change.
scoreSummary: DEVELOPMENTS IN LITERACY INC shows exceptional impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.dil.org/donate (Source: https://www.dil.org/donate)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Strong donor fit, low risk profile, and credible supporting evidence for outcomes.

all_citations.claim: DIL holds a 100.0/100 score, an 89.8% program expense ratio, 15.6 months of working capital, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.04 per $1 raised.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and provides a policy for Zakat donations.
Total revenue was $2,785,991 and the organization operates schools and teacher education programs.
DIL tracks student dropout rates and the transition of graduates to higher education or vocational training.
The organization monitors Matric Board Exam pass rates (A/A+) as a metric of educational quality.
DIL offers various donation options and giving methods for supporters.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: DIL earns a strong overall rating due to its perfect financial health score and significant direct impact on global education. The organization demonstrates high data confidence and exceptional operational efficiency.
areas_for_improvement: Medium donor alignment score suggests opportunities to further tailor engagement for specific donor segments
Continued focus on reducing student dropout rates in remote regions [6]

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows medium alignment with donor expectations, balancing a strong track record in a high-urgency cause—global education—with established Zakat-eligibility for Muslim donors [2][9]. DIL has operated since 1997, providing a stable and trusted platform for philanthropic investment in Pakistan's youth [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: DIL demonstrates high impact through capacity building, maintaining a low cost per beneficiary while managing a vast network of 214 schools [3][6]. The organization focuses on long-term outcomes such as student transitions to higher education and vocational training [6][8]. With total revenue of $2,785,991, DIL directs 89.8% of its expenses toward its core educational programs [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Developments in Literacy (DIL) empowers underserved children in Pakistan through high-quality education and teacher training to break the cycle of poverty.
strengths: Exceptional financial efficiency with a program expense ratio of 89.8% [1][3]
Strong liquidity with 15.6 months of working capital available [1]
Highly efficient fundraising operations costing only $0.04 per $1 raised [1]
Proven track record in student outcomes, including high Matric Board Exam pass rates [8]
baseline_narrative.summary: founded in 1997, Developments in Literacy (DIL) operates 214 schools serving 89,433 students across Pakistan, with a specific focus on increasing educational access for girls [3][6]. The organization maintains a perfect Charity Navigator score of 100.0/100, reflecting its commitment to financial transparency and accountability [1]. DIL is also Zakat-eligible, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations through educational empowerment [2].

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust DIL due to its <cite id="3">100.0/100 from Charity Navigator score</cite> and <cite id="13">Platinum Candid Seal</cite>. The board consists of <cite id="16">89.8% independent members</cite>, ensuring objective oversight.

Seek to support established educational infrastructure in Pakistan

impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: DIL empowers communities by providing high-quality, low-cost education that prepares students for higher education and vocational success.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking transition rates ensures that students are not just enrolled, but are actually progressing through the educational system.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With <cite id="1">89,433 students</cite> already served, DIL has the infrastructure to scale its ed-tech and teacher training programs to more government schools.

strengths.detail: DIL operates with a <cite id="6">89.8% program expense ratio</cite> and spends only <cite id="7">$0.05 to raise every dollar</cite>.
The organization holds a <cite id="3">perfect 100/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite> across all beacons.
DIL reports that <cite id="11">84% of students successfully transition</cite> from primary to middle and middle to secondary school.

strengths_deep_dive: DIL's impact is rooted in its ability to scale quality education at a remarkably low cost. The organization achieves an <cite id="12">estimated cost of $4.55 per beneficiary</cite>, which is considered exceptional for the global education sector. Beyond basic literacy, they invest heavily in human capital, providing <cite id="4">extensive teacher development</cite> to ensure long-term sustainability. Their commitment to transparency is further validated by a <cite id="13">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1997, Developments in Literacy (DIL) has evolved into a major educational force in Pakistan, operating <cite id="1">214 schools serving 89,433 students</cite>. The organization focuses on breaking the cycle of poverty by providing <cite id="2">free, high-quality education to underserved children</cite>, with a strategic emphasis on gender equity. DIL maintains <cite id="3">a perfect 100/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, a rare distinction that signals top-tier accountability and financial health. Their model is built on <cite id="4">teacher training and localized curricula</cite>, ensuring that educational quality remains high even in remote regions. Financially, the charity is exceptionally lean, reporting <cite id="5">total revenue of $2,440,822</cite> with a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 89.8%</cite>. This efficiency is matched by a <cite id="7">fundraising efficiency of $0.04 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that nearly every dollar donated directly supports classroom activities and student outcomes. For Muslim donors, DIL offers a <cite id="8">verified zakat-eligible giving option</cite>, specifically targeting the educational needs of the poor and needy (fuqara and masakin). By combining <cite id="9">ed-tech initiatives like TEAL</cite> with traditional classroom support, DIL addresses the digital divide while maintaining a <cite id="10">low student dropout rate of only 5%</cite>.
